source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --peer-auto-tls argument is not set to true

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that the --peer-auto-tls argument is not set to true
input_row.description: Do	not	use	automatically	generated	self-signed	certificates	for	TLS	connections	between peers.
input_row.rationale: etcd	is	a	highly-available	key	value	store	used	by	Kubernetes	deployments	for	persistent storage	of	all	of	its	REST	API	objects.	These	objects	are	sensitive	in	nature	and	should	be accessible	only	by	authenticated	etcd	peers	in	the	etcd	cluster.	Hence,	do	not	use	self- signed	certificates	for	authentication. Impact: All	peers	attempting	to	communicate	with	the	etcd	server	will	require	a	valid	client certificate	for	authentication.
input_row.audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	etcd	server	node: ps -ef | grep etcd Verify	that	if	the	--peer-auto-tls	argument	exists,	it	is	not	set	to	true. Note: This	recommendation	is	applicable	only	for	etcd	clusters.	If	you	are	using	only	one etcd	server	in	your	environment	then	this	recommendation	is	not	applicable.
input_row.remediation: Edit	the	etcd	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml	on	the	master node	and	either	remove	the	--peer-auto-tls	parameter	or	set	it	to	false. --peer-auto-tls=false Default	Value: 150	|	P a g e Note: This	recommendation	is	applicable	only	for	etcd	clusters.	If	you	are	using	only	one etcd	server	in	your	environment	then	this	recommendation	is	not	applicable. By	default,	--peer-auto-tls	argument	is	set	to	false. References: 1. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/security.html 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/etcd/ 3. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/configuration.html#peer-auto-tls Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 151	|	P a g e
input_row.impact: All	peers	attempting	to	communicate	with	the	etcd	server	will	require	a	valid	client certificate	for	authentication.
input_row.references: 1. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/security.html 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/etcd/ 3. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/configuration.html#peer-auto-tls Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 151	|	P a g e
input_row.justification: This control requires verification of the etcd process arguments—specifically, that `--peer-auto-tls` is not set to true. Alibaba Cloud APIs, Aliyun CLI, and Alibaba Cloud Config do not expose the runtime process arguments or the contents of the etcd manifest file on the underlying nodes, especially in managed Kubernetes (ACK) clusters. Accessing this information requires direct inspection on the server via SSH or similar means, which cannot be automated through Alibaba Cloud’s native API or CLI tools.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. **Access the etcd Server Node:** - SSH into the master node where the etcd process is running. This may require identifying the node via the Alibaba Cloud Console or using `kubectl get nodes` if you have cluster access. 2. **Inspect the Running etcd Process:** - Run the command: `ps -ef | grep etcd` to list all running etcd processes and their arguments. 3. **Check the --peer-auto-tls Argument:** - Review the command line output for the presence of the `--peer-auto-tls` argument. - If present, ensure it is set to `false`. If the argument is absent, the default is `false` (secure). 4. **Confirm Compliance:** - The node is compliant if `--peer-auto-tls` is not present or explicitly set to `false`. - Document the findings for audit purposes.
input_row.default_value: 150 | P a g e Note: This recommendation is applicable only for etcd clusters. If you are using only one etcd server in your environment then this recommendation is not applicable. By default, --peer-auto-tls argument is set to false .
input_row.additional_information: This control cannot be audited and modified in ACK managed clusters.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control requires verification of the `etcd` process arguments, specifically whether `--peer-auto-tls` is not set to true. This particular examination necessitates SSH access to the master node of the etcd server where these process arguments are defined. The Alibaba Cloud APIs, Aliyun CLI, and Alibaba Cloud CLI do not provide visibility into runtime process arguments or the contents of the etcd manifest file, especially in Alibaba's managed Kubernetes (ACK) clusters. Therefore, direct inspection is required, making this a manual audit.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the etcd Server Node:**
   - SSH into the master node where the etcd process is running. You may need to identify the node via the Alibaba Cloud Console or using `kubectl get nodes` if you have cluster access.

2. **Inspect the Running etcd Process:**
   - Run the command: `ps -ef | grep etcd` to list all running etcd processes and their arguments.

3. **Check the --peer-auto-tls Argument:**
   - Review the command line output for the presence of the `--peer-auto-tls` argument.
   - If present, ensure it is set to `false`. If the argument is absent, the default is `false` (secure).

4. **Confirm Compliance:**
   - The node is compliant if `--peer-auto-tls` is not present or explicitly set to `false`.
   - Document the findings for audit purposes.
